response.justification: Candidate brings strong geospatial domain expertise and senior-level credentials, but lacks the primary requirement: modern C++ or Rust experience. Only 6 months of outdated C++ work from 2014-2015 and no Rust exposure. Recent career has been full-stack web and product engineering rather than systems-level development. However, demonstrated ability to master multiple languages, high-performance systems thinking (low-latency work in DLang), and genuine routing/navigation domain knowledge through GISCollective and Green Map work suggest potential with ramp-up time.
strengths: Deep geospatial and navigation domain expertise from founding GISCollective and building web GIS applications for Green Map, directly relevant to routing tile pipelines
Proven track record building high-performance, low-latency distributed systems at scale (millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby using async I/O and fibers)
Senior-level engineer with exceptional ability to learn new languages and tech stacks (C#, Swift, DLang, Java) and strong emphasis on code quality, testing, and infrastructure
concerns: C++ experience is 10+ years outdated with no modern C++11/14/17/20 or production systems work; no Rust experience despite being listed as required alternative, and C++ not even listed in skills section

Recent career trajectory is full-stack web and product engineering, not systems-level development; distributed systems experience is application-level (microservices, APIs) rather than OS-level fault tolerance and consistency patterns

Geospatial work is mapping/GIS focused (Node.js/Ember.js) rather than routing/navigation systems; no evidence of working with navigation engines, routing algorithms, or production routing tile infrastructure

key_factors.factor: No modern C++ or Rust skills (primary job requirement)
Strong geospatial/routing domain expertise and high-performance systems thinking
Career gap between web-stack and systems-level development focus
